findings.title: Persona and pipeline role align tightly with documented dimensions
findings.detail: Quinn-R is positioned as the final automated gate, separates judgment from delegated automation (quality-control), and threads medical-education context, calibration, and independence from content creation consistently through Identity, Principles, and activation flows. This reduces user confusion about when to invoke the agent versus Irene or Marcus.

findings.title: Five quality dimensions are operationalized end-to-end
findings.detail: Review protocol, feedback-format dimension table, and SKILL overview all enumerate the same five dimensions with matching severity semantics and escalation rules for content accuracy. That alignment supports coherent reports and predictable routing to Marcus and humans.

findings.title: Degradation and partial-review behavior is explicit
findings.detail: Style bible, scripts, course context, and incomplete artifacts each have a defined fallback or SKIP pattern, which keeps the agent usable under missing dependencies without silently pretending full coverage.

findings.title: Instructional soundness lacks a discrete capability row
findings.detail: SKILL.md lists QA, CC, BV, LA, FG, and SM while instructional soundness is a full dimension in review-protocol and feedback-format. Implementers scanning only the capability table might assume that dimension is out of scope or handled elsewhere.
findings.action: Add an IS (or equivalent) row pointing to review-protocol.md for instructional soundness, or annotate QA as covering all five dimensions explicitly.

findings.title: Four capability codes route to the same review protocol file
findings.detail: QA, CC, BV, and LA all load ./references/review-protocol.md. The distinct codes suggest four separate procedures but progressive disclosure is actually one protocol with steps; users may wonder which code to use first.
findings.action: Document that these codes are aliases or phases of one workflow, or merge into a single capability with sub-bullets per dimension.

findings.title: CC label 'Compliance checking' is easy to misread as umbrella compliance
findings.detail: In the protocol, brand and accessibility are separate dimensions with their own names, while CC suggests broad regulatory or policy compliance. New readers may map CC to BV+LA+accessibility together rather than primarily automated/judgment accessibility and related checks.
findings.action: Rename CC to something scope-specific (e.g., Accessibility & WCAG compliance) or add a one-line gloss under the table tying CC to the accessibility column in review-protocol.

findings.title: quality-control skill is required but not declared as an install prerequisite in-agent
findings.detail: Activation and protocol assume scripts under quality-control (accessibility_checker, brand_validator, quality_logger). In a repo that only contains bmad-agent-quality-reviewer, headless runs would hit degradation paths unless another skill supplies those assets.
findings.action: Add a short Prerequisites or Companion skills subsection listing quality-control and key paths, or link to where those scripts must live.

findings.title: Save Memory is thin relative to rich memory-system rules
findings.detail: save-memory.md summarizes checkpoints while memory-system.md carries access boundaries, triggers, and mode rules. Cohesion is fine, but a single consolidated 'when to call SM' checklist could reduce the chance the executor skips chronology or patterns updates.
findings.action: Extend save-memory.md with bullet triggers cross-linked to memory-system sections, or add a flowchart note in SKILL.md under SM.

agent_identity.name: bmad-agent-quality-reviewer
agent_identity.persona_summary: Quinn-R, meticulous QA specialist for educational/medical artifacts, final gate before human review, structured severity and calibration with Marcus.
agent_identity.primary_purpose: Systematic multi-dimension quality validation of production artifacts with structured reports and optional persistence via quality-control and sidecar memory.

persona_alignment.notes: Expertise (accessibility, brand, objectives, instructional soundness, flagged accuracy) matches protocol and feedback formats; tone and reporting rules match the QA guardian role.

missing_areas: Explicit instructional-soundness entry in the SKILL capability table
In-skill declaration of quality-control as a required companion for full automation
capability_completeness.notes: Core review, reporting, memory, and external automation hooks are present; minor table and prerequisite clarity gaps only.

consolidation_opportunities.suggested_consolidation: Treat as one 'Run review protocol' capability with dimension tags, or state clearly that all four codes load the same file for different mental models.

external_integration.notes: quality-control scripts and SQLite logging are documented with context passed; redirect to bmad-help is consistent with orchestration by Marcus.

user_journey_score.notes: Headless and interactive paths, partial review, and escalation to human for medical flags form a coherent journey; standalone use needs companion quality-control assets.

summary.single_most_important_fix: Clarify the internal capability table: either add instructional soundness explicitly and rename or scope CC, or merge QA/CC/BV/LA into one documented workflow entry so users do not expect four separate procedures.
